A universal polymer signature in Hi C resolves cohesin loop density and supports monomeric extrusion Kirill Polovnikova,b,1 ID and Dmitry Starkova
The genome is folded by molecular motors, yet how many motors are required to generate a single chromatin loop has remained unknown
The three dimensional organization of chromosomes is central to gene regulation, replication, repair, and segregation (1)
Significance Loop extrusion is believed to dynamically fold meter long DNA inside a micrometer sized nucleus, yet the density of these loops has remained unknown
